description: Fix inconsistency in plugin-development.mdx where the 'Understand the Plugin Class' code example omits `override` on `displayName`, while the scaffolder output, the first-plugin tutorial, and the resources-prompts guide all use `override readonly displayName`. Although `displayName` is abstract (so `override` is technically optional), the inconsistency confuses developers who compare the docs to scaffolded code and wonder which form is correct. The fix is to add `override` to match the scaffolder and all other docs pages.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add `override` to displayName in plugin-development.mdx code example
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the plugin class code example in the plugin-development guide to match what the scaffolder generates, so that I don't wonder whether `override` is needed on `displayName`.
acceptanceCriteria: In plugin-development.mdx, the 'Understand the Plugin Class' code example (~line 85) changes from `readonly displayName = 'My App';` to `override readonly displayName = 'My App';`
No other content in the file changes
The change is consistent with the scaffolder output (platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts:359), first-plugin.mdx:64, and resources-prompts.mdx:499
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx, line 85

Current code (inside a ```typescript fenced code block):
```
  readonly displayName = 'My App';
```

Change to:
```
  override readonly displayName = 'My App';
```

This is a single-word addition in a code example. The surrounding lines should not change.

For reference, the consistent pattern used everywhere else:
- Scaffolder (platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts:359): `override readonly displayName = ...`
- first-plugin.mdx:64: `override readonly displayName = 'My App';`
- resources-prompts.mdx:499: `readonly displayName = 'Project Tracker';` — wait, check this one too. If it also lacks override, fix it in the same change.

Actually, check ALL code examples in docs/content/docs/ that show `readonly displayName` on a plugin class and ensure they all use `override readonly displayName` for consistency. Use grep to find them:
  grep -rn 'readonly displayName' docs/content/docs/

Fix any that are missing `override`.
